channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Cursor’s 25-year-old CEO is a former Google intern who just inked a $60 billion deal with SpaceX
item.description: From Google intern at 18 to a billionaire at 25, Cursor CEO Michael Truell's rise is one of Silicon Valley's fastest.

item.title: A Popular Fast Food Chain Just Went All-In on MAHA With Its Latest Hire
item.description: Steak ’n Shake has appointed its first-ever “Make America Healthy Again” officer.

item.title: This new EV battery from CATL can get to an 80% charge in under 4 minutes
item.description: A new battery from Chinese company CATL, the world’s largest electric vehicle battery manufacturer, can be fully recharged in under seven minutes. Cha...

item.title: Keep Up With Payroll Compliance
item.description: Payroll mistakes are costly. Here’s how to stay compliant as the requirements become more complex.

item.title: Anthropic Investigates ‘Unauthorized Access’ to Its Ultra-Powerful Claude Mythos AI Model
item.description: Meanwhile, CISA, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Agency, reportedly does not have access to Claude Mythos.

item.title: Google’s $750 Million Plan to Move AI From Experiments to the Real World
item.description: Google’s new strategy aims to solve one of AI’s biggest problems.

item.title: Best Buy CEO Corie Barry is stepping down: How she went from architect of a comeback to cautionary tale
item.description: Shares are up only 6% since she was appointed in 2019, vastly underperforming the S&P 500.

item.title: Visa CMO: AI agents are your new customers — here’s how to sell to them
item.description: As AI takes over consumer decision-making, companies must rethink everything from product data to brand purpose — or risk being invisible.

item.title: Federal appeals court blocks California law requiring federal agents to wear badges or ID because it ‘attempts to directly regulate the U.S.’
item.description: The three-judge panel said the law violated the Constitution. California lawyers say the law applied evenly to all law enforcement officers.

item.title: Starbucks Sent Real Assistants to ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ Premiere—in Costume
item.description: The marketing stunt sparked praise, jokes, and backlash online.

item.title: David’s Bridal exec has a warning for every CEO obsessed with AI’s return-on-investment
item.description: David's Bridal CBO Elina Vilk says companies chasing a discrete AI return-on-investment are asking the wrong question—and missing the real opportunity...

item.title: Yesway IPO: Stock price will be closely watched today as the convenience store chain makes its Nasdaq debut
item.description: While its geographic footprint is all west of the Mississippi, convenience store operator Yesway Inc. is making New York City headlines this week with...

item.title: This fast-food chain just hired a “Chief MAHA Officer”
item.description: A burger joint obsessed with beef tallow fries is going all-in on the MAHA agenda. Steak ‘n Shake, a fast casual burger destination with 391 locations...

item.title: How being honest about the process of ‘becoming’ leads to success
item.description: The great jazz trumpeter Miles Davis once said, “Sometimes you have to play a long time to be able to play like yourself.” This is why the best artist...

item.title: Everyone says this movie is terrible and it’s still about to make $70 million
item.description: Michael is aiming for a record-breaking opening weekend. It’s also a critical flop. In a vacuum, the Michael Jackson biopic might sound like a perfect...
